name: Python
creator: Guido van Rossum
description: Python is a high-level, versatile programming language favored for its readability and ease of use. It is widely used in automation, data science, web, and scripting.
usedFor: Web development, data science, scripting, automation, machine learning.
funFact: Python is named after the British comedy group Monty Python.

terminalCommands.install: Download from python.org or use: pip install

name: JavaScript
creator: Brendan Eich
description: JavaScript is the programming language of the web, primarily used to create interactive effects within browsers.
usedFor: Web development, front-end scripting, backend with Node.js.
funFact: JavaScript was created in just 10 days.

name: TypeScript
creator: Anders Hejlsberg
description: TypeScript adds optional static typing to JavaScript, enabling safer and more predictable code.
usedFor: Large-scale JavaScript applications, web development, tooling.
funFact: TypeScript compiles to plain JavaScript, so it can run anywhere JavaScript runs.

name: C
creator: Dennis Ritchie
description: C is a general-purpose programming language that provides a foundational base for many modern languages. It is known for performance and control.
usedFor: Operating systems, embedded systems, system programming.
funFact: The Unix operating system was written in C.

terminalCommands.install: Install GCC from mingw (Windows) or via package manager (Linux/Mac).

topics.title: Variables
topics.explanation: Variables in C are named memory locations. You must declare the type of variable before using it.

keyTerms.definition: Specifies the variable name and type.
Assigning a value at the time of declaration.

topics.challenge: Declare an integer variable 'age' and assign it 21.
topics.terminalNote: Compile with gcc file.c -o file; run with ./file

topics.title: Arrays
topics.explanation: Arrays store multiple values of the same type in contiguous memory locations.

keyTerms.definition: A collection of variables of the same type.
Position of element in the array, beginning from 0.

topics.title: Pointers
topics.explanation: A pointer is a variable that stores the memory address of another variable.

keyTerms.definition: Variable that holds a memory address.
Access the value a pointer points to.

commonMistakes: Using wrong types in pointer assignment.
topics.challenge: Declare a pointer to int named 'iptr'.

topics.title: Functions
topics.explanation: Functions group code into reusable units. You must declare a return type and parameters.

keyTerms.definition: Named section of code that performs a task.
Variable used to receive data inside function.

topics.challenge: Write a function multiply that returns product of two ints.
topics.challengeHint: Return int, take two int parameters.

topics.title: Structs
topics.explanation: Structs are custom data types that group variables under one name.
codeExample: struct Point { int x; int y; };
solution: struct Car { char model[20]; int year; };
commonMistakes: Accessing members with dot operator incorrectly.
topics.challenge: Declare a struct Student with name and grade.
topics.challengeHint: Use char array for name, int for grade.
challengeSolution: struct Student { char name[50]; int grade; };

topics.title: File IO
topics.explanation: C allows reading and writing files using FILE pointer and functions like fopen, fprintf, fscanf.

keyTerms.definition: Pointer to structure controlling a file.

commonMistakes: Not checking if file opened successfully.
topics.challenge: Open a file data.txt for appending.

topics.title: Memory
topics.explanation: C gives direct control over memory allocation with malloc and free.

keyTerms.definition: Releases memory back to the system.

topics.title: Strings
topics.explanation: Strings in C are arrays of characters ending with null character.

topics.title: Loops
topics.explanation: Loops are used to repeat a block of code for a certain number of times or until a condition is met.

keyTerms.definition: Repeats code a specific number of times.
Repeats code while a condition holds.

topics.challenge: Print numbers 1 to 5 using a loop.

topics.title: Preprocessor
topics.explanation: The C preprocessor runs before compilation, handling tasks like including files, defining macros, and conditional code.
topics.challenge: Define a macro SQUARE(x) that multiplies x by itself.

name: C++
creator: Bjarne Stroustrup
description: C++ extends the C language with object-oriented, generic, and functional features. It is used for performance-critical applications.
usedFor: System/software, games, real-time, embedded, high-performance computing.
funFact: C++ was originally named 'C with Classes'.

terminalCommands.install: Install g++ from mingw (Windows) or via package manager (Linux/Mac).

topics.title: Variables
topics.explanation: Variables store data in C++. You must specify the type explicitly for strong type safety.
topics.challenge: Declare a float variable 'temp' with value 98.6.

topics.title: Arrays
topics.explanation: Arrays in C++ are collections of fixed-size elements of the same type. Variables are ordered in memory.

name: C#
creator: Anders Hejlsberg, Microsoft
description: C# is a modern, object-oriented language designed for flexibility and productivity on the .NET platform.
usedFor: Desktop/web/mobile apps, Unity games, cloud services.
funFact: C# is heavily influenced by both C++ and Java.

topics.title: Variables
topics.explanation: Variables in C# store information. Type must be specified or inferred using 'var'.
topics.challenge: Declare a double variable price with 9.99.

topics.title: Arrays
topics.explanation: Arrays hold several values of the same type in a single variable.
topics.challenge: Create a string array of 3 cities.
